issue_body:
Add an audio polish step to the episode workflow so creators can choose simple quality goals before producing the episode. The step should present understandable options for noise cleanup, voice leveling, speech clarity, and overall enhancement, with per-speaker indicators tied to the imported tracks. It should feel like choosing the desired sound quality, not configuring technical audio processing.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A creator can move from episode setup into an Audio Polish step, see each assigned speaker track, choose from clear quality presets such as Natural, Clean, and Studio, adjust noise cleanup, leveling, speech clarity, and enhancement with simple controls, and see a saved summary of the chosen audio treatment in the episode review/export path. Existing setup, style preview, and canvas editor flows still work, and `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes with tests covering the new audio polish flow.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The product already has import/setup, visual preset selection, and a reusable canvas editor. The next missing foundation for a publishable long-form episode is clean, balanced audio presented in creator-friendly terms, which directly advances the core promise before adding more advanced contextual editing or export features._
